findings.title: Dropping cache token counts regresses reported usage and context percentage
findings.description: `StreamEvent::Result.input_tokens` is consumed downstream as the total prompt-side token count for both the Discord summary (`format_tokens`) and context utilization (`format_ctx_suffix`). This change stops including `cache_creation_input_tokens` and `cache_read_input_tokens`, so turns that rely on prompt caching will now under-report token usage and show an artificially low `ctx:%`. The existing test case demonstrates the regression: a result with 26,147 cached prompt tokens would now be reported as only `3` input tokens.
findings.suggestion: Keep aggregating `input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens` for the existing `input_tokens` field, or introduce separate fields if you need both raw input-only and total prompt-token metrics without changing current bot behavior.

summary: One behavioral regression found: the diff undercounts prompt-side token usage by excluding cached tokens from parsed result metrics.
